SEARCH
Package compiler.lexer

Class Token

java.lang.Object
compiler.lexer.Token
public class Token extends Object
Represents a single lexical token extracted from source code. Tokens are immutable and carry four essential pieces of information:
  • Type: the grammatical category (keyword, operator, literal, etc.)
  • Lexeme: the exact text as it appeared in source
  • Line & Column: source position for error reporting
  • Value: pre-parsed numeric form (for number tokens only)
The parser receives an ordered list of tokens and makes decisions based on type and lookahead, without needing to re-scan source text. Position information is preserved for diagnostics.
See Also:
  • TokenType
  • Lexer.tokenize()

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Token(TokenType type, String lexeme, int line, int column)
    Convenience overload for non-numeric tokens (keywords, operators, identifiers).
    Token(TokenType type, String lexeme, int line, int column, int value)
    Full constructor carrying both textual and numeric forms.

  • Constructor Details

    • Token

      public Token(TokenType type, String lexeme, int line, int column)
      Convenience overload for non-numeric tokens (keywords, operators, identifiers). Initializes the numeric value to 0 since these token types do not represent numbers.
      Parameters:
      type - token category (non-null)
      lexeme - token text as it appeared in source (non-null)
      line - source line number (1-based)
      column - source column number (1-based)

    • Token

      public Token(TokenType type, String lexeme, int line, int column, int value)
      Full constructor carrying both textual and numeric forms. For number tokens, the value is pre-parsed during lexing to avoid re-parsing in the parser. For non-numeric tokens, value is typically 0.
      Parameters:
      type - token category (non-null)
      lexeme - token text from source (non-null)
      line - source line number (1-based)
      column - source column number (1-based)
      value - pre-parsed numeric value (0 for non-numeric tokens)

  • Method Details

    • getType

      public TokenType getType()
      Returns the token type (category). The parser uses this to make dispatch decisions about how to handle the token.
      Returns:
      the token type (never null)

    • getLexeme

      public String getLexeme()
      Returns the original source lexeme text.
      Returns:
      original text, useful for preserving user-facing formatting and messages

    • getLine

      public int getLine()
      Returns the source line number.
      Returns:
      line metadata for precise diagnostics

    • getColumn

      public int getColumn()
      Returns the source column number.
      Returns:
      column metadata for precise diagnostics

    • getValue

      public int getValue()
      Returns the numeric payload associated with this token.
      Returns:
      pre-parsed numeric payload to avoid repeated string-to-int conversion
